title: How He Made $16,969 In 30 Days Starting A YouTube Channel

transcript.text: You can see I've made over 2 million, which is very nice. >> What I call them? Fun coupons. >> I'm back to my brokey days. So, So, I only started posting about like the end of May. And um so far, it's I was able to revive the channel to a decent point, which it is in right now. And um so far, for the in the past 28 days, um I've been able to get it back up to like 15 plus K. >> What caused this um this growth? >> Um I found out about your program on Twitter. And I was like I wanted to go into like the 3D niche, um which I will still get back to, but for now, I'm just like getting myself back on my feet. >> So, what was the most valuable part of coaching [music] for you? Like, how do you see Art of YouTube? And uh what was the most part [music] valuable part of it for you? >> Yeah, I think for me, >> All right, everybody. And I'm from Nigeria. >> Nice. Do you mind uh sharing right away uh sharing how much money you've generated in the last months, just to set up a tone for for the whole um interview? Maybe you can even share your screen, kind of show the explosive growth of the channel, so that we start talking about results right away. >> Yeah, let me show you um the analytics of the channel. >> Sure. >> All right, so this is the channel, and I it's like a dead channel, as you can see um past 90 days. So, there was no activity on the channel for this long. So, I only started posting about like the end of May. And um so far, it's I was able to revive the channel to a decent point, which it is in right now. Congrats. >> Yeah, actually one of the biggest things in my 3 years of YouTube. So, we I'm actually like really happy about this channel. >> Yeah. >> So, it's >> Very nice. And can you go back to your YouTube studio? Do you mind changing the currency to euro just so people see that it's actual like revenue that uh that you've generated as US dollar? >> Okay. Um >> Yeah, just go to settings. Yeah, and then just change it to euro so that people can uh can see that it's actually US dollar and not >> Okay, so So, if I go back to um what's the currency you usually use? Jamaican >> Euro. >> Um >> Or yeah, Jamaican dollar. Yeah, let's let's show some crazy >> Yeah, you can see I've made over 2 million, which is very nice. But, if I switch it back over to US, you can see um Where's the US? >> It's a little bit down. Uh >> All right, you can see I'm back to my brokie days. So, it's it's All right, so yeah, this it's >> So, US dollars 16, 17,000 dollars and basically the channel only started posting at the at the end of May. So, it's been pretty much a month since you started posting. Can you walk me through the story? Like why did you decide to revive this channel? And did you change the niche? Like what caused this this growth? Like how did you take it from zero, literally zero, to 17,000 dollars a month? >> Okay. So, initially I was just running a some commentary niche, which which is still what I'm running, but at the time I was just running like the lowest possible quality commentary niche. >> Okay. >> just like the first over and just like cutting clips. And then I wanted to do something branded. So, I found out about your program on Twitter. And I was like I wanted to go into like the 3D niche, which I will still get back to, but for now I'm just like getting myself back on my feet. But I wanted to go go into the 3D niche. And um during my time of transitioning to the 3D niche, a thing or two happened. I got caught up in the whole you know authentic wave. So, I couldn't sustain that channel anymore. So, I went back into like doing the commentary, but this time I wanted to do it things differently. And I got I've gotten a little bit of information like through the program like concerning like how to kind of like not copy and like steal like the skeletons of like viral formats, so you can be able to create your own original content. >> Mhm. >> Um so, some of those knowledge and also like some of the know- knowledge of like how how to use like this is like the main one. Like it's how to use like CloudCode, right? To kind of like create your own videos like in in terms of helping you to script and find ideas. Right now, I'm only using it for scripting. So, with that information, I was able to like Initially, I was scared of CloudCode, but like the program made it like easier for me to kind of get familiar with it. So, I I took some of the protocols which you gave, right? I was able to use those protocols to kind of like set it up in a way that I adapted it to my own niche, right? Then, I was able to use it to write like better scripts through like the CloudCode protocols you gave us. >> Yeah. >> And yeah, that's that's how I was able to like get the channel back up because I didn't use my previous method or my previous ways of running commentary channel. Right? So, yeah. >> Makes sense. Basically, for for people watching, just to kind of walk them through the whole system. So, before, what did not work for you is that you used to like copy directly like what worked for competitors. So, you just literally like steal their scripts, their ideas. But the whole system that we run is basically how do you take the skeleton of it? How do you like understand what works and only like replace the idea with something original, but then follow the exact format that worked because I'm a strong believer that content is based on frameworks. You can see the framework and you can copy the framework without literally like stealing the piece of content, without stealing someone else's video. And you can just replace it with a fresh angle, new idea, and you can make it yours. So, that's literally what you did and now we're now doing it with CloudCot. We are automating the entire ideation process and like scripting process as well. So, you know use Cloud Court only for scripting, right? >> Yes, right now I'm using it for only scripting. I know that there's so much more I can use it for like when I find viral videos with my competitors. Is there a way I can do that? For now, I'm just using it for scripting. I know there's so much more opportunity there. >> Yeah, we built the entire dashboard that will soon release that will allow you to see all of the videos from your competitors with the exact angles how you can adapt them stealing the framework but like keeping the the ideas original and this will be a huge help for automation automating the entire process for you because something about those simple channels something that we are planning to do with you now is like the moment you scale them to significant numbers which you already did, you want to reinvest that cash in something more branded something that runs for longer. So, let's say 2D 3D animation etc. So, you can build those channels now with all of the capital that you have acquired through a very simple niche and you basically run this channel for as long as it you can like you you milk it for as long as it possible so that it generates you not 17k but like 50k so that you can then deploy this capital into something that will run for years not months because definitely like do you think that what what do you think is going to happen to to your niche now like how do you see the potential of the channel like how much money you think you can generate over the next like 3 months with it? >> Yeah, like yeah, for the lifespan of the channel like you said actually I I remember hearing when you said you consigned like a channel a 3D channel that you was in your portfolio. Um you said like since you guys started running the channel like so far you guys have gotten like one competitor, right? Yeah, and since I started running my channel initially when I started, I had like maybe three big competitors. Right now that I have up to like 11. And this is like just in a month. >> Yeah. >> So, definitely it's the saturation is the main issue with like um the the lifespan of channels like this. So, sustainability is not something I'm really looking for. >> Mhm. >> Instead, it's just to get the best out of the channel and deploy it into something more >> Yeah. >> long term. >> Yes. >> Yeah. >> Exactly. So, for now we'll just optimize the team so you put out more content than your competitors and then just reuse all of this cash to to build something a long term and something branded. And what I like about commentary guys is that you have the skill of spotting viral ideas and you learn the skill of using Cloud Coach to automate that. So, now it's not going to be a problem for you to uh to do it in a more branded complicated niche. Um but I wanted to ask you, you've been a client of mine for a while now. I think we've worked together for like three or maybe like four months. Uh so, what was the most valuable part of coaching for you? Like how do you see Art of YouTube and what was the most part valuable part of it for you? >> Yeah, I think for me the most valuable part apart from like the like the one-on-ones, I would say is and this actually was like something I really wasn't really expecting, right? It was like more in detailed concerning the use of AI. >> Yeah. >> Um for your like creating YouTube like for your creating content, right? Like you guys go in depth about how to actually use AI. I think it's one of the most valuable thing I've gotten from the program so far. Yeah. >> Love it. Love it. And uh what do you think about the the whole process? Was smooth for you? Was everything clear? You said you were scared about Cloud Coach for a little bit. So, kind of walk me through your like client experience. How did it feel? >> Okay, honestly, when I joined, um I was very skeptical concerning like um uh you know, like like will this 3D niche work for me? Like uh I know like it's I'm thinking like it's going to go slow. Well, I think I would have made it successful you know of Runway, but that's by the wayside. But, initially I was skeptical about like oh like is the information in this course going to be applicable if I don't end up doing 3D? >> Mhm. >> Right? So, uh which I ended up like still going through and I still use all the information and some of the resources to be able to adapt it to my own commentary niche. Even though it's not necessarily what I planned, but I was still able to use the information. Um >> Nice. >> something else. >> Nice. I love that. And uh what do you think uh like what would you say to someone who's currently doubting that uh they can make it on YouTube? Let's say someone is uh about your age thinking of like seeing all of those case studies online thinking all of the money that YouTube can make, but if they're still like would that work for me? Can I make it work? What would you say to to this person? >> Yeah, I would say like um honestly, it's it's it's not like an overnight thing. It's not something like you can just like go into and be like, "Okay, um I'll I'm just going to make it work in like um two three months." It does happen, but like it's rare. I'll say like give it like six months before you like kind of start like saying, "Oh, this won't work for me." or "This will work for me." And um you can also start on your own. You don't need um to um get some resources. You can start on your own, try things out. That way you will better know if when you are looking for something to help you out, you will know what to look for. And yeah, so just if you can find help to to speed up the process, definitely by all means. Like when I wanted to go into 3D, I didn't want to figure out anything about the hiring. Like it was just That was the first thing I was making me not want to even start 3D. And when I saw your coaching program, I was like, "Okay, like this is just something I can Yes, save me the time." So if you are in that um position as well, like go for it. You can You can like find help to accelerate your process. But yeah, just don't um don't think it's an overnight thing. I just like give it some time um to see some results. >> Nice. I love it. 6 months is typically what it takes to for a complete beginner to start seeing um some original results. I like the saying by Mr. Beast that uh your first 100 videos are going to suck. Uh post 100 videos and then we'll talk. So it's basically like I don't think that you need 100 videos, especially if you get some some extra help and you understand strategy. But overall, it's true in a way that no matter how good you are at studying, you need practice to literally like understand and get a skill of posting content because essentially it's a skill. So uh yeah, it does take time, it does take repetition until you finally dial in the framework in a way that you can generate 17K in the first month of like reviving the channel. So uh yeah. I think you're going to absolutely destroy it. So, uh looking forward to recording another interview maybe like 3 4 months down the line with another channel. Uh, but uh yeah, man. Uh, everyone who's watching, I'll see you in the next one.
